title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. subbifidiforme
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. subbifidiforme, also known as woodland hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been reported to aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, providing health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, as well as supporting digestive and immune system health. Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. subbifidiforme can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves for topical application, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made from this versatile plant.
